test_cases.input: Original: dense academic narration about urban planning history. Target voice: journalist-presenter style, investigative tone, slightly adversarial. Sample transcript provided from target presenter.
test_cases.notes: Voice transformation was clear and consistent. Factual content was fully preserved. The adversarial register was applied appropriately without distorting the factual register. Voice Notes accurately described the structural changes.

test_cases.input: Original: dry corporate-style narration for a broadcaster-requested natural history segment. Target: warm, intimate, wonder-driven. No sample provided.
test_cases.notes: Voice transformation worked well without a sample — the description was sufficient for the register. Warmth was applied without becoming sentimental. One fact was slightly paraphrased in the adaptation; Voice Notes flagged this for the producer to check.

test_cases.input: Original: first-person presenter narration. Target: third-person, more detached, for a different version of the same film with a different presenter. The target voice has no prior sample.
test_cases.notes: First-to-third person transformation required more structural changes than simple re-voicing. The adapted version maintained factual integrity. Voice Notes correctly flagged that the emotional subjectivity of the original does not translate cleanly to a detached third-person register.
